title: Savings embedded in electricity bills + Working capital embedded in freight booking
description: EAM's Savin embeds investment into electricity bills, Visa and Transcard embed working capital into WebCargo, FLEET220 launches ODOS for unified fleet payments, Triffin embeds £100 million in lending into AI finance workflows.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: HotelRunner's $4B travel payments bet + Lyft finally launches a prepaid wallet
description: HotelRunner launches Ruby for B2B travel payments and Lyft introduces Cash Rewards wallet. Latest embedded finance news on non-financial brands building financial services.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Allica Bank buys Kriya + Wayflyer's platform play
description: Allica Bank acquires embedded lending platform Kriya. Wayflyer launches Hosted Capital for platform financing. MrBeast files trademark for MrBeast Financial.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Worldpay vs. Stripe & Adyen: Can they catch up in embedded finance?
description: Worldpay launches embedded finance with Unit to challenge Stripe and Adyen. Plus Reverb's marketplace wallet strategy with Ansa. Deep dive analysis.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Virtual event: Co-brands vs EF + Why Sibill's Italy launch matters
description: Register for our co-brand vs embedded finance debate with David Parker. Also: Why Sibill's Italian embedded banking launch is breaking new ground in Europe.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Odoo's Global Expense Card Launch: 15M Users, 175 Countries - The Biggest Embedded Banking Launch Yet?
description: Odoo launches global expense cards for 15 million users via Stripe partnership, while Gusto expands into embedded finance with credit and bill pay. Plus: Club Brugge's innovative fan debit card launch.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: While You Were Away: Wix Launches Financial Services, Booking.com's 6% Cashback Card, and More
description: Catch up on the latest embedded finance news: Wix's banking and lending launch, Booking.com's 6% cashback credit card with Imprint, and Pleo's investment feature. Analysis of the payments-to-banking strategy.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Your embedded INVESTMENT and INSURANCE summer reading: What happened in 2025 (so far)
description: Summer recap of embedded investment and insurance developments in 2025. Key acquisitions, new partnerships, and why non-financial brands are embracing embedded insurance while investment infrastructure consolidates.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Your embedded PAYMENT summer reading: What happened in 2025 (so far)
description: Explore embedded payment trends in 2025: How ToolTime and WeTransfer boosted revenue with payment products, plus infrastructure provider updates from Adyen, Ryft, and more.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Three announcements that aren't embedded finance but should be on your radar
description: Porsche switches credit card providers to Nordiska Bank & Enfuce, Nordea partners with Mynt for SME expenses, and ClearScore launches embedded marketplace. Why these moves matter for embedded finance.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Adyen Doubles Down on Embedded Finance; Wolt's Fintech Journey
description: Adyen Co-CEO reveals 63% embedded finance revenue growth strategy while Xero acquires payments fintech Melio for $3 billion to compete with QuickBooks in the US market.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Bad Week for Embedded Finance? ING-Amazon Split & Intergiro License Loss Explained
description: Embedded finance reality check: Why ING-Amazon split, what Intergiro's license loss means, and how European payment providers are fighting back.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: My Virtual Event Experiment Continues + ToolTime's Embedded Payments Growth Signals
description: Following up on my first virtual embedded finance event success (26/37 attendees!) plus analyzing ToolTime's €2B invoice volume and what Adyen's case study reveals about embedded payments adoption in Germany's craft industry.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Why telecoms are giving up on banking (but e-commerce isn't)
description: From Orange Bank's exit to Mercado Libre's digital banking ambitions and Sage's embedded accounting expansion - why e-commerce beats telecoms in finance.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Join the Debate: Retailers in Embedded Finance | Lending Moves from Glovo & Ageras
description: Join our first virtual event on retailers in Embedded Finance. Plus, read about new embedded lending deals from Glovo and Ageras — and what Seccl’s funding says about the future of embedded investing.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: eBay's embedded lending offering is coming to Germany
description: eBay partners with YouLend for German merchant lending, Juuli launches freelancer credit cards, and former B2C fintech Auric transforms into an infrastructure provider. Plus industry insights and upcoming events for embedded finance professionals.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Beyond Software: allO and Circula's Embedded Finance Journey
description: Explore how restaurant software provider allO and expense management platform Circula are transforming their business models through innovative embedded finance solutions, blurring the lines between traditional software and financial services.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Pliant's Insurance Card Play & Rakuten Acquires BaaS Technology
description: Pliant acquires Hi.Health to expand into insurance card solutions, while Rakuten picks up Dock Financial’s BaaS technology for a potential European push. BKN301 raises €21.5M to grow its Banking-as-a-Service platform from MENA to Europe. Plus: Thokmandee launches B2B BNPL for wholesale, and HBX Group debuts a B2B eWallet for the travel industry.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Three banks show how to enter Embedded Finance: Partner, build or invest
description: Discover how major banks are revolutionizing embedded finance through partnerships, dedicated divisions, and strategic investments. Plus, insights on Xero's multi-approach banking strategy.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Stablecoins vs. BaaS: The battle of 2025? Help me write embedded finance history!
description: Everybody is talking about stablecoins, but what does this mean for traditional banking-as-a-service models? X is preparing its payment launch, and I am seeking your insights on the last five years of embedded finance development. Plus, our latest podcast shows how banks can fight back in embedded finance.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: My personal 2024 recap: Growing Embedded Finance Review from a newsletter into a multi-channel platform
description: Dive into Embedded Finance Review's 2024 journey: from growing my newsletter subscriber base by 70% to launching a podcast reaching 41 countries. Learn about my successes, lessons learned, and exciting plans for 2025, including international events and new sponsorship opportunities.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Railsr is back!
description: British banking-as-a-service provider Railsr has a new chance to win embedded finance; Danish Lunar spins out its infrastructure unit; and Dutch banking frontend provider Flow partners with Adyen.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: What's the price for Solaris' new lifeline?
description: British BaaS Griffin partners with investment provider WealthKernel; Banxware and UniCredit increase their partnership; Banking Circle is for sale and Mambu acquires Numeral.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Doctolib enters embedded finance
description: Doctolib partners with Adyen, HR provider Deel acquires a fintech startup to improve its payroll product, and HSBC's new POS financing solution allows British consumers to pay for solar panels in instalments.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Is banking-as-a-service provider Solaris for sale?
description: Solaris is looking for fresh funding but is also exploring a sale; SEB Embedded announces new partnership; and Cambodia is using fintech to increase tourism spending.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Is orchestration the new black in embedded lending?
description: This edition covers orchestration in embedded lending, a new Singapore-Saudi Arabia embedded finance partnership, and the right way to price your embedded payment product offering.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Barclays completes Tesco Bank acquisition | Brazil wants to regulate BaaS
description: In the newest podcast episode, I am speaking with Saible's CEO Jarvey Moss about why he decided to fix payments in the construction industry. Additionally, I cover the completion of Barclay's Tesco Bank acquisition, Shopify's new financial hub and the development in the US sponsor bank market.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Does every bank need an embedded finance strategy?
description: Why is embedded finance so much bigger in the US compared to Europe? What other embedded product is lending provider Pipe launching? Which US bank launched a new embedded finance brand?
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Two British retailers launch new embedded finance products
description: The department store chain John Lewis partnered with Zopa for a consumer embedded lending product and electronic retail chain Currys partnered with BNP Paribas to upgrade its existing BNPL product. Also, Shopify, TikTok, and Mister Spex announce new embedded finance products.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: HSBC launches embedded finance joint venture & UK investment-as-a-service provider expands to the EU
description: The British bank shares more details about its new joint venture with Tradeshift; in the newest podcast episode, we speak about the key ingredients for a successful embedded finance product and WealthKernel becomes the first investment-as-a-service provider with licences in the UK and EU.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Swedish retailer partners with SEB Embedded & Hilton launches two debit card products
description: Three European non-financial brands have launched new banking products, each with their own twist. Swedish retailer Hemköp needs to prove that its offering is attractive enough; Hilton's choice of issuing partner is a surprise; and the Spanish subsidiary of a property technology provider targets a new type of customer.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Solaris shuts down ex-Contis business and Brex goes embedded
description: In this edition, we learn about how embedded finance works in Pakistan, why Solaris shuts down the former Contis business, how the Brex Embedded offering is different to other providers plus more infrastructure providers, and other insightful news stories.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: All eyes on the invisible companies in embedded finance
description: Being an infrastructure provider is not easy. If things go well, nobody knows you. But if things go wrong... This week, three European banking-as-a-service providers and an US wallet provider have taken the stage and positioned themselves for new business. As a non-financial brand, you should know what they offer and how they differ.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Fintech meets healhtech | How virtual cards can improve back office processes
description: Holiday Extras partners with checkout.com for a new virtual card solution, Xero extends their partnership with Modulr to lauch a payroll product and is Swedish lending provider Froda eyeing their first non-financial brand?
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Why did Swiss retailer Coop shut down its embedded finance product? What can other retailers learn from it?
description: We discuss Coop's decision to pull the plug on their banking product in our latest podcast. Additionally, ClearBank secures a Dutch banking licence, what will this mean for EU embedded finance? A new report brings some light on the rise of new infrastructure provider and Amazon launches co-branded credit cards in the UK and Germany.
author: Embeddedfinancereview

title: Stripe adds a new banking partner. Why this is big news for embedded finance?
description: Stripe partners with Fifth Third Bank for its treasury product, Sage partners with Stripe in the UK for an embedded payment product, Doordash extends its fintech offering from cards into payments and five learnings for banks from the Synapse bankruptcy.
author: Embeddedfinancereview
